attacking_style: Launches aggressive, classics-style attacks with 3-4km to go on punchy finales, excelling in sustained solo efforts that exploit TT power. Particularly dangerous on hilly stage race finales rather than pure sprint or mountain stages.
team_tactics: Versatile role: elite domestique setting high tempo on climbs (Paterberg), but elevated to aggressor when given freedom—initiates multiple late accelerations (sub-4km) forcing rivals to chase repeatedly and inverting sprint freshness dynamics.
terrain_preference: Gravel sectors and punchy hilly finales (3-5min efforts) suit best. Strong on sustained climbs in stage racing contexts (Paris-Nice GC contention). Exposed on technical high-speed descents and when positioning is lost before decisive splits.
vulnerability: Poor initial positioning costs him repeatedly—missed decisive splits at Omloop 2020 despite favorite status. Technical descending under extreme pace (Lombardia). Lacks final acceleration for podiums when surviving selections (Flanders 2020: 10th).
key_patterns: Attacks with 3-4km remaining multiple times, particularly effective in hilly finales exploiting TT power
Demonstrates gravel/Strade Bianche specialization with multiple podiums (2017, 2019, 2023) across career span
Paris-Nice GC contender pattern: 2020 near-miss, 2021 attack forcing Roglič response, 2020 stage win
Positioning errors cost results: missed Omloop split 2020, dropped Lombardia descent 2021, crashed Flanders 2022
Role versatility: elite domestique tempo-setting (E3 2022) vs. late-race aggressor (Dwars 2022 multiple accelerations)
Recovery ability shown: third Tirreno six days after Strade win (2018), sustained form blocks
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows inconsistency—Strade podium and LBL 5th demonstrate retained punch, but Dauphiné inability to follow HC selections suggests ceiling on sustained climbing remains. Leadership crash at Flanders 2022 disrupted classics trajectory.
built_from: 2022 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
